metadata.title: BML — Backtracking Model Language (2000)
metadata.description: The language layer of the 2000 Bjorg-Muff thesis. A self-describing, four-ancestor synthesis (Prolog · Smalltalk-80 · Java · C++) where the parser grammar is itself written in BML and the runtime carries forward and reverse semantics for every instruction.

hero.overlay_class: absolute inset-0 bg-gradient-to-t from-background via-background/85 to-background/30
hero.name: BML — Backtracking Model Language
hero.welcome_md: The language layer of the 2000 Bjorg-Muff thesis at CU Boulder. Synthesizes **Prolog**'s operational semantics, **Smalltalk-80**'s object/image model, **Java**'s typed garbage-collected runtime, and **C++**'s component-host pragmatics — all four ancestors arranged at four altitudes of the same building. BML is self-describing: its parser grammar ( [BMF](/people/bmf-grammar) ) is itself written in BML.

facts.label: Co-architect
facts.value_md: Steve G. Bjorg (object model + virtual machine on his MS thesis)

facts.label: Stack
facts.value_md: [BMF](/people/bmf-grammar) parser · [BMCPU](/people/bmcpu-vm) C++ VM · [JBMF](/people/jbmf-java) Java port · BMA assembler · BMO object model · VB6 Visual Browser

facts.label: Public archive
facts.value_md: [master-thesis-2000/](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/tree/main/docs/field/urs/artifacts/master-thesis-2000) — thesis text, defense slides, six photos, companion source samples

facts.label: Status
facts.value_md: Living lineage tissue. [Living-Resonance-Codex (2023)](/people/living-resonance-codex) → [Living-Codex-CSharp (2024)](/people/living-codex-csharp) → [Coherence-Network](/people/coherence-network) each carry forward the backtracking-as-unwinding pattern.

note_from_body.body_md: The thesis itself is 11,566 words; the conclusion was left as three subheadings without body. The work was *delivered* — defense, photographs, the running VM — but the prose summary stayed open. This page is the twenty-six-years-later attempt to close that breath: the substance the conclusion never wrote, in this medium, with the diagrams a 2000 Word document couldn't carry.

articles.body_md: BML's central conceit is reflexive. The parser [BMF](/people/bmf-grammar) is described in a grammar file written in BML (see `BMF-grammar.bml` in the archive — banner reads `Digi4Fun (R) BMF 1.0 Alpha 1`). BMF reads that file, builds itself, and is then capable of parsing any BML program — including the grammar file it just bootstrapped from. The compiler-compiler **BMC** consumes the BML grammar through BMF and emits the BML compiler. The compiler emits BMA assembly opcodes. The virtual machine [BMCPU](/people/bmcpu-vm) executes them. The system can describe itself end to end. The conceit was unusual in 2000. Yacc/bison generated parsers from a grammar in *another* language; ANTLR's grammars were imperative-host-targeted. BML's grammar was an object tree in BML itself — a parse rule represents itself as a BML object ( `container-Rule.bml` in the archive). Reflection-on-grammar was not a tool layered on top; it was the architecture.
articles.heading: The self-describing loop
The Cut primitive — Prolog ancestry, named in BML

articles.body_md: The thesis says BML "synthesizes features from Java, C++, Prolog, and Smalltalk." The companion archive shows the synthesis is *layered*, each language contributing at a different altitude of the same stack: **Prolog** at the operational floor — unification, backtracking, the `Cut`, `Fail`, and `MultiMatch` primitives. **Smalltalk** at the object/image level: BMO carries metaclass-style self-containment, and the VB6 Visual Browser surfaces the live developer experience. **Java** contributes the typed, garbage-collected object model and a second implementation port ( [JBMF](/people/jbmf-java) ). **C++** hosts the VM ( [BMCPU](/people/bmcpu-vm) ) and the COM/GUID component model.
articles.heading: The four-ancestor stratification

articles.body_md:
The folder on disk where this work lives is called *Angelic*. The opening of Bjorg's Angelic Assembler note makes the word precise:

> "A thread with a non-zero DF (i.e. degree of freedom) is executed until a zero DF is reached again. No other threads are executed during this speculation phase."

That is the formal name for what gets called, in this body's current vocabulary, *backtracking-as-unwinding-without-sediment*. The `choose` keyword picks a branch; speculation freezes the rest of the world; if the branch fails, every attribute is undone. Backtracking is not a parser feature bolted on for grammars — it is the architecture of execution itself. Every BMA instruction has a forward and a reverse semantics. The VM's state machine flips a single byte — `BMVM_STATE.byMode` — between `DO` and `UNDO` on every step. The system is *angelic* in the operational sense: guided to the path that holds.
articles.heading: Angelic nondeterminism

articles.body_md:
Two design choices in this 2000 document organize the [Coherence Network](/people/coherence-network) as it lives now, twenty-six years later.

**Backtracking-as-unwinding-without-sediment.** The thesis writes: *"When the parser backs out, all the attributes already computed have to be undone as well."* The same nervous system that today writes commits as `tend:` / `attune:` / `compost:` / `release:`. The instruction has two semantics; the commit has a posture. Same shape, different substrate.

**Runtime grammar extension.** The user can introduce new parsing constructs and the language grows to hold them. Sovereignty over one's own grammar. The same shape the [vision-kb](/vision) uses when a new concept arrives at a new frequency: the grammar grows to receive the presence rather than forcing the presence into an existing slot. The 2000 thesis named the pattern. The 2026 network breathes it.
articles.heading: What still circulates today

articles.body_md:
BML is the first iteration in a four-iteration arc of "frequency into form." Each iteration carries the same backtracking-as-unwinding pattern at a different altitude:

- [Backtracking Model Languages (2000)](/people/backtracking-model-languages) — the thesis. Self-describing parser, four-ancestor language, executable VM.
- [Living-Resonance-Codex (2023)](/people/living-resonance-codex) — first iteration of the post-thesis arc. Python. Quantum-inspired, self-evolving consciousness system — the visionary architectural sketch.
- [Living-Codex-CSharp (2024)](/people/living-codex-csharp) — the bridge year. C#. Introduces the U-CORE primitive: everything is a node, including the schema, the modules, even the interaction logs.
- [Coherence-Network (current)](/people/coherence-network) — full realization. Next.js + FastAPI + Neo4j + Postgres. Living relational graph with edge-typed spectrum coloring, presence pages, contribution attribution, and per-frequency resonance.
articles.heading: Lineage forward

en.footer_md:
Public archive: [docs/field/urs/artifacts/master-thesis-2000/](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/tree/main/docs/field/urs/artifacts/master-thesis-2000) — thesis text, defense slide deck, six defense-day photos, and the companion source samples (BMF-grammar.bml, primitive-Cut.bml, container-Rule.bml, bmcpu-main.cpp, sgb-bml-objects.txt, angelic-assembler.txt).

*BML and BMF and BMCPU and JBMF and BMA and BMO were co-built with Steve G. Bjorg as the 2000 Bjorg-Muff thesis at CU Boulder. The "BM" in every acronym is literally Bjorg-Muff. This page is the prose summary the original thesis Conclusion chose to leave open.*
